message:
fix(widgets/progress): right-align MiniBar percent under essential-only degradation

When rendering at `DegradationLevel::EssentialOnly` or above,
`MiniBar` skips the decorative bar and only draws the percent.
The previous implementation always drew the trimmed percent
string at `area.x`, which left a visually jarring left-aligned
number in a right-edge-anchored slot and broke layouts that rely
on the percent being flush with `area.right()`.

The rendering path now computes `pct_width = display_width(&pct)`
and, when `area.width >= pct_width`, places the text at
`area.right().saturating_sub(pct_width)` so the rightmost glyph
of the percent sits exactly on `area.right() - 1` without padding
beyond the area. When the area is too narrow to hold the full
three-digit percent, it falls back to the existing trimmed
left-aligned behavior so a 2-cell slot can still show `"5%"`
etc.

A new unit test `minibar_essential_only_right_aligns_percent_when_width_allows`
builds a 7-cell area under `EssentialOnly`, renders a 50%
`MiniBar`, and asserts that the first three cells are blank and
the last four hold `" 50%"`, locking in the right-alignment
contract for future regressions.
